ogTitle: Pakenham South vs Pakenham Upper: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Pakenham South and Pakenham Upper.
heading: Pakenham South vs Pakenham Upper — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Pakenham South, Victoria and Pakenham Upper, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Pakenham South has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Pakenham Upper (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Pakenham South or Pakenham Upper safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Pakenham South has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Pakenham Upper scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Pakenham South scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Pakenham Upper scores 28/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Pakenham South or Pakenham Upper?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Pakenham South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.98% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
